mnemonics: SIFT through your HOG features to find the right ORB for the CORNER job.
keyPoints: Feature extraction identifies and describes distinctive points (keypoints) in an image for tasks like matching and recognition.
Common algorithms include SIFT (Scale-Invariant Feature Transform), HOG (Histogram of Oriented Gradients), and ORB (Oriented FAST and Rotated BRIEF), each with different strengths.
The goal is to create a robust descriptor for each feature that is invariant to changes in scale, rotation, and illumination.
commonMistakes: Confusing feature extraction with feature detection; detection finds the points, extraction describes them.
Assuming all feature descriptors (e.g., SIFT, HOG) are invariant to all transformations; each has specific strengths and weaknesses (e.g., HOG is not rotation-invariant).
Using a default set of parameters for an algorithm (like the number of keypoints) for every image, which can lead to poor performance on different datasets.
